steps.title: Match corresponding angles
steps.content: The similarity statement says triangle $CAE$ is similar to triangle $CBD$. The vertex order gives the correspondence $C$ to $C$, $A$ to $B$, and $E$ to $D$. So angle $CAE$ corresponds to angle $CBD$.

steps.title: Use similarity
steps.content: Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent. The side relationship $AE=24(BD)$ affects lengths, not angle measures, so the measure of angle $CAE$ is $56^\circ$. The answer is B.
